name: Window COMP
description: The Window Component allows you to create and maintain a separate floating or fixed window displaying the contents of any Panel or any Node Viewer.
summary: The Window Component allows you to create and maintain a separate floating or fixed window displaying the contents of any Panel or any Node Viewer.

parameters.name: Window Operator
parameters.label: Window Operator
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.label: Title
parameters.description: title - Specify the window's title.

parameters.name: Justisy and Offset to...
parameters.label: Justisy and Offset to...
parameters.description:
justifyoffsetto -  - All the positioning parameters below are done relative to the location you specify here. Your window can span more than the specified 'area', it's just used as the reference for positioning. Note for macOS: When using 'Bounds of all Monitors' you must turn off the 'Displays have separate Spaces' toggle in System Preferences > Desktop & Dock > Mission Control, see Multiple_Monitors#macOS for details.

 primarymonitor - The primary monitor which is sometimes referred to as the main display in Windows control panel or the primary display in the NVIDIA control panel. specifymonitor - Defines the location to be the monitor specified in the Monitor parameter below. allmonitors - Defines the location to include all monitors. The TaskBar is ignored when using this option.

parameters.name: Primary Monitor
parameters.label: Primary Monitor
parameters.description: primarymonitor - The primary monitor which is sometimes referred to as the main display in Windows control panel or the primary display in the NVIDIA control panel. specifymonitor - Defines the location to be the monitor specified in the Monitor parameter below. allmonitors - Defines the location to include all monitors. The TaskBar is ignored when using this option.

parameters.name: Ignore Taskbar
parameters.label: Ignore Taskbar
parameters.description: ignoretaskbar - The Windows taskBar is ignored when this option is 'On'. When off the taskbar is accounted for so position and sizing will not cover it up.

parameters.name: Monitor
parameters.label: Monitor
parameters.description: monitor - Specify the monitor index when  is set to Single Monitor.

parameters.name: Justify Horizontal
parameters.label: Justify Horizontal
parameters.description:
justifyh -  - Aligns the window horizontally with the monitor or bounds of all monitors.

 left - Align window so that left edge coincides with left edge of specified area. center - Align window so that horizontal center coincides with horizontal center of specified area. right - Align window so that right edge coincides with right edge of specified area. mouse - Align window so it opens horizontally centered on the mouse cursor.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.label: Left
parameters.description: left - Align window so that left edge coincides with left edge of specified area. center - Align window so that horizontal center coincides with horizontal center of specified area. right - Align window so that right edge coincides with right edge of specified area. mouse - Align window so it opens horizontally centered on the mouse cursor.

parameters.name: Justify Vertical
parameters.label: Justify Vertical
parameters.description:
justifyv -  - Aligns the window vertically with the monitor or bounds of all monitors.

 top - Align window so that top edge coincides with top edge of specified area. center - Align window so that vertical center coincides with vertical center of specified area. bottom - Align window so that bottom edge coincides with bottom edge of specified area. mouse - Align window so it opens vertically centered on the mouse cursor.

parameters.name: Top
parameters.label: Top
parameters.description: top - Align window so that top edge coincides with top edge of specified area. center - Align window so that vertical center coincides with vertical center of specified area. bottom - Align window so that bottom edge coincides with bottom edge of specified area. mouse - Align window so it opens vertically centered on the mouse cursor.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
winoffset -  - Horizontal offset applied to window after justifying.

 winoffsetx - Horizontal offset applied to window after justifying. winoffsety - Vertical offset applied to window after justifying.

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: winoffsetx - Horizontal offset applied to window after justifying. winoffsety - Vertical offset applied to window after justifying.

parameters.name: Shift to Single Monitor
parameters.label: Shift to Single Monitor
parameters.description: single - This menu has options for shifting the opening window. You can either shift to a single monitor or shift to the monitor the cursor is over when the window opens.

parameters.name: DPI Scaling
parameters.label: DPI Scaling
parameters.description:
dpiscaling -  - Options for managing DPI scaling on high DPI monitors. To inspect a monitor's DPI scaling setting, you can use the Monitors DAT and refer to the dpi_scale column.

 native - Uses the full resolution of the monitor's native pixels, regardless of the operating system's display scaling setting for that monitor. ie Display Scale = 1.0 usedpiscale - Uses the resolution set by the operating system's display scaling setting for that monitor. For example, a 3840x2160 monitor with display scaling set to 2.0 results in an addressable resolution of 1920x1080. On Windows system this would be a Display Scaling setting of 200%.

parameters.name: Native
parameters.label: Native
parameters.description: native - Uses the full resolution of the monitor's native pixels, regardless of the operating system's display scaling setting for that monitor. ie Display Scale = 1.0 usedpiscale - Uses the resolution set by the operating system's display scaling setting for that monitor. For example, a 3840x2160 monitor with display scaling set to 2.0 results in an addressable resolution of 1920x1080. On Windows system this would be a Display Scaling setting of 200%.

parameters.name: Opening Size
parameters.label: Opening Size
parameters.description:
size -  - Determines how the size of the window is determined.

 automatic - Determines the size automatically from the size of the / specified. fill - Fills the location specified in the  parameter above. custom - Use the  and  parameters below to specify a custom size.

parameters.name: Automatic from Panel COMP/TOP
parameters.label: Automatic from Panel COMP/TOP
parameters.description: automatic - Determines the size automatically from the size of the / specified. fill - Fills the location specified in the  parameter above. custom - Use the  and  parameters below to specify a custom size.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.label: Width
parameters.description: winw - The width of the window when Opening Size parameter is set to Custom.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.label: Height
parameters.description: winh - The height of the window when Opening Size parameter is set to Custom.

parameters.name: Update Settings from Window
parameters.label: Update Settings from Window
parameters.description: update - When the window is open you can reposition and resize it. Clicking this button will then read its current windows settings and apply them to the parameters above.

parameters.name: Borders
parameters.label: Borders
parameters.description: borders - Controls whether or not the window contains borders and a title bar.

parameters.name: Include Borders in Size
parameters.label: Include Borders in Size
parameters.description: bordersinsize - When 'On' the borders are included in the size of the window.

parameters.name: Always on Top
parameters.label: Always on Top
parameters.description: alwaysontop - Controls whether or not the window always sits atop other floating windows.

parameters.name: Cursor Visible
parameters.label: Cursor Visible
parameters.description:
cursorvisible -  - Controls whether or not the cursor remains visible when over this window.

 nocursor - The cursor is never visible over the window. cursoronmove - The cursor will only be visible when moving and for a short period after it stops moving. alwaysvisible - The cursor will always be visble when over the window.

parameters.name: Never
parameters.label: Never
parameters.description: nocursor - The cursor is never visible over the window. cursoronmove - The cursor will only be visible when moving and for a short period after it stops moving. alwaysvisible - The cursor will always be visble when over the window.

parameters.name: Close on Escape Key
parameters.label: Close on Escape Key
parameters.description: closeescape - When 'On' pressing the escape key over this window will close it.	TIP: Shift-Escape will always close it, whether this parameter is on or off.

parameters.name: Allow Viewer Interaction
parameters.label: Allow Viewer Interaction
parameters.description: interact - Enables interactions with the operator specified in the  Operator parameter.

parameters.name: Allow Minimize
parameters.label: Allow Minimize
parameters.description: allowminimize - Enables the window to be minimized in the taskbar (dock in macOS).

parameters.name: V-Sync Mode
parameters.label: V-Sync Mode
parameters.description:
vsyncmode -  - Controls how the window is updated with regards to V-Sync. Enabled means it will update in sync with the monitors refresh which avoids tearing and lost frames. Disabled means it can update at any point during the refresh which can result in tearing or lost frames.  is Half Monitor Rate should be used when doing things such as running a 30fps file on a 60Hz display. This makes each update be shown for exactly 2 refreshes which keeps motion looking smooth.

 disabled - Vertical Sync off. enabled - Vertical Sync on. halfmonitorrate - This allows you to run your project at 30FPS on a 60Hz monitor, but ensuring every image is shown for exactly 2 refreshes. Without this option an image may be shown for 1 or 3 refreshes, which makes the content look like it is stuttering. Currently this feature is not functional in the 2022.20000+ series of builds, but we hope to bring back this feature when Vulkan adds support for it.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.label: Disabled
parameters.description: disabled - Vertical Sync off. enabled - Vertical Sync on. halfmonitorrate - This allows you to run your project at 30FPS on a 60Hz monitor, but ensuring every image is shown for exactly 2 refreshes. Without this option an image may be shown for 1 or 3 refreshes, which makes the content look like it is stuttering. Currently this feature is not functional in the 2022.20000+ series of builds, but we hope to bring back this feature when Vulkan adds support for it.

parameters.name: Draw Window
parameters.label: Draw Window
parameters.description: drawwindow - When disabled the window will not update it's contents at all. Useful for processes that arn't doing rendering such as Audio or networking processes, or for when using VR devices.

parameters.name: Hardware Frame-Lock
parameters.label: Hardware Frame-Lock
parameters.description: hwframelock - Provides multi- frame-lock sync using Nvidia Quadro Sync and AMD S400 sync cards. For more information, see Syncing Multiple Computers and Hardware Frame Lock.

parameters.name: OpenGL Stereo
parameters.label: OpenGL Stereo
parameters.description: openglstereo - No longer supported. Turn 'On' when using openGL stereoscopic output.

parameters.name: Right Eye Operator
parameters.label: Right Eye Operator
parameters.description: winrightop - No longer supported. This parameter is enabled when the OpenGL Stereo parameter above is turned on. Specify the Camera COMP used for the right eye here.

parameters.name: Open as Perform Window
parameters.label: Open as Perform Window
parameters.description: performance - Opens this   in Perform Mode. Any   can be set as default Perform  (opens using F1 shortcut) using the Window Placement Dialog. This button allows you to open this   in  without changing what is currently selected as the 'default' Perform .

parameters.name: Open as Separate Window
parameters.label: Open as Separate Window
parameters.description: winopen - Opens this   as its own floating window, not as the Perform . Useful for things such as dialog boxes, popups, or testing, but should not be used for putting final rendered content to outputs. Use a single large Perform  for that instead of separate windows.

parameters.name: Close
parameters.label: Close
parameters.description: winclose - Closes the window, if it's open.

parameters.name: Set as Perform Window
parameters.label: Set as Perform Window
parameters.description: setperform - Permanently changes the Perform  setting in the  Placement dialog to this window.

parameters.name: Window Placement Dialog
parameters.label: Window Placement Dialog
parameters.description: opendialog - A shortcut to open the  Placement dialog.

parameters.name: Include in Placement Dialog
parameters.label: Include in Placement Dialog
parameters.description: includedialog - When 'On' this   will be displayed in the  Placement .

parameters.name: Extension
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description:
ext - Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component



  ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.


    
  ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.



  ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: Promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: reinitextensions - Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: Parent Shortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: parentshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See Parent Shortcut.

parameters.name: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: opshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See Global OP Shortcut.

parameters.name: Internal OP
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description:
iop - Sequence header for internal operators.



  iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.



  iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: OP
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Operator Viewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: opviewer - Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: Enable Cloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: enablecloning - Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's ' Master'.

parameters.name: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: enablecloningpulse - Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: Clone Master
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: clone -  to a component used as the Master Clone.

parameters.name: Load on Demand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: loadondemand - Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: enableexternaltox - When on (default), the external  file will be loaded when the  starts and the contents of the  will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external  on startup if desired (the contents of the  are instead loaded from the  file). Useful if you wish to have a  reference an external  but not always load from it unless you specifically push the Re-Init Network parameter button.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: enableexternaltoxpulse - This button will re-load from the external  file (if present).

parameters.name: External .tox Path
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: externaltox -  to a  file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the  file can not be found, whatever the  file was saved with will be loaded.

parameters.name: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: reloadcustom - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's Custom Parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: reloadbuiltin - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Save Backup of External
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: savebackup - When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External  parameter is saved in the  file.  This backup copy will be used if the External  can not be found. This may happen if the  was renamed, deleted, or the  file is running on another computer that is missing component media.

parameters.name: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: subcompname - When loading from an External  file, this option allows you to reach into the  and pull out a  and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For example if a  file named project1 contains project1/geo1, putting geo1 as the Sub- to Load, will result in geo1 being loaded in place of the current . If this parameter is blank, it just loads the  file normally using the top level  in the file.

parameters.name: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description:
relpath -  - Set whether the child file paths within this  are relative to the  itself or the , or inherit from parent.

 inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.

parameters.name: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.label: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.description: inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.
